calibration.near_region: initial standardized distance d <= 5 at the frozen positive-only checkpoint

mechanism_diagnostics_at_near_retention_.interpretation: Under matched initial average near retention, faster tail decay preserves less harmful far influence and is associated with higher finite-horizon held-out-context reward.

allowed_claims: Near-retention matching succeeded within the preregistered tolerance.
Taper shape still changed harmful-far retention and finite-horizon held-out-context reward after matching average initial near retention.
All three faster-decaying candidate families had positive paired reward deltas against reciprocal-linear on 20 of 20 formal seeds at retention 0.75.
Squared-distance exponential is the strongest candidate in this frozen finite-horizon matrix.

artifact_provenance.note: This compact deposition records the completed formal summary and claim boundary. It does not fabricate or replace the missing raw-complete artifact; raw trajectories and their checksum must be restored from the original delivered result package before archival publication.
